Ōmine Head Shrine ([[Shugendō]])
[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)
Tenkawa Benzaiten, Tsubouchi Benzaiten Shrine
[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = [[:en:July 17|July 17]] | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "[[:en:Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten]]" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubushima Shrine]]) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]]. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]], who prayed for victory during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional [[:en:Noh|Noh]] theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual [[:en:July 16|July 16]]–[[:en:July 17|July 17]] festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From [[:en:Shimoichiguchi Station|Shimoichiguchi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442752]] Wikidata name: Tenkawa Daibenzaiten-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha 01.jpg | location = [[:en:Tsubouchi|Tsubouchi]], [[:en:Tenkawa|Tenkawa]], [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Latitude degrees = 34| Latitude minutes = 13| Latitude seconds = 24.0 | Longitude degrees = 135| Longitude minutes = 50| Longitude seconds = 30.0 | deity = [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Yoshino General Shrine
Ōmine Head Shrine ([[Shugendō]])
[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)
Tenkawa Benzaiten, Tsubouchi Benzaiten Shrine
[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = [[:en:July 17|July 17]] | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "[[:en:Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten]]" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubushima Shrine]]) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]]. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]], who prayed for victory during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional [[:en:Noh|Noh]] theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual [[:en:July 16|July 16]]–[[:en:July 17|July 17]] festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From [[:en:Shimoichiguchi Station|Shimoichiguchi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

By bus: From [[:en:Shimoichiguchi Station|Shimoichiguchi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11442752}}

Ōmine Head Shrine ([[Shugendō]])
[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)

[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = [[:en:July 17|July 17]] | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "[[:en:Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten]]" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubushima Shrine]]) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]]. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]], who prayed for victory during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional [[:en:Noh|Noh]] theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual [[:en:July 16|July 16]]–[[:en:July 17|July 17]] festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From [[:en:Shimoichiguchi Station|Shimoichiguchi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442752}}

[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)
Tenkawa Benzaiten, Tsubouchi Benzaiten Shrine
[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = [[:en:July 17|July 17]] | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "[[:en:Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten]]" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubushima Shrine]]) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]]. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]], who prayed for victory during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional [[:en:Noh|Noh]] theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual [[:en:July 16|July 16]]–[[:en:July 17|July 17]] festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From [[:en:Shimoichiguchi Station|Shimoichiguchi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Ōmine Head Shrine ([[Shugendō]])
[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)
Tenkawa Benzaiten, Tsubouchi Benzaiten Shrine
[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = [[:en:July 17|July 17]] | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "[[:en:Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten]]" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubushima Shrine]]) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]]. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]], who prayed for victory during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional [[:en:Noh|Noh]] theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual [[:en:July 16|July 16]]–[[:en:July 17|July 17]] festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From [[:en:Shimoichiguchi Station|Shimoichiguchi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - [[:en:Yoshino-Kumano National Park|Yoshino-Kumano National Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

**Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From {{ill|Shimoichiguchi Station|en|Shimoichiguchi Station|WD=Q7496967|lt=Shimoichiguchi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - {{ill|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|en|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|WD=Q1204533|lt=Yoshino-Kumano National Park}} == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11442752]] Wikidata name: Tenkawa Daibenzaiten-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha 01.jpg | location = {{ill|Tsubouchi|en|Tsubouchi|WD=Q16879527|lt=Tsubouchi}}, [[:en:Tenkawa|Tenkawa]], [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude degrees = 34| Latitude minutes = 13| Latitude seconds = 24.0 | Longitude degrees = 135| Longitude minutes = 50| Longitude seconds = 30.0 | deity = [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Yoshino General Shrine
Ōmine Head Shrine ([[Shugendō]])
[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)
Tenkawa Benzaiten, Tsubouchi Benzaiten Shrine
[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = {{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}} | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "{{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|en|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|WD=Q17214976|lt=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}}" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubushima Shrine}}) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja}}, who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}}. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}, who prayed for victory during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh}} theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}–{{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}} festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen}} (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From {{ill|Shimoichiguchi Station|en|Shimoichiguchi Station|WD=Q7496967|lt=Shimoichiguchi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - {{ill|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|en|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|WD=Q1204533|lt=Yoshino-Kumano National Park}} == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "{{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|en|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|WD=Q17214976|lt=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}}" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubushima Shrine}}) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja}}, who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}}. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}, who prayed for victory during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh}} theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}–{{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}} festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen}} (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From {{ill|Shimoichiguchi Station|en|Shimoichiguchi Station|WD=Q7496967|lt=Shimoichiguchi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - {{ill|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|en|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|WD=Q1204533|lt=Yoshino-Kumano National Park}} == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - {{ill|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|en|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|WD=Q1204533|lt=Yoshino-Kumano National Park|1=Yoshino-Kumano National Park}} == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha]][[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha 01.jpg | location = {{ill|Tsubouchi|en|Tsubouchi|WD=Q16879527|lt=Tsubouchi|1=Tsubouchi}}, [[:en:Tenkawa|Tenkawa]], [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Latitude degrees = 34| Latitude minutes = 13| Latitude seconds = 24.0 | Longitude degrees = 135| Longitude minutes = 50| Longitude seconds = 30.0 | deity = [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank = Yoshino General Shrine
Ōmine Head Shrine ([[Shugendō]])
[[Gōsha]] (local shrine) in Shinto | consecration_year = Unknown | Main hall = | Alternate name = Ōmine Head Shrine, Yoshino-Kumano Shrine (intermediate between Yoshino and Kumano)
Tenkawa Benzaiten, Tsubouchi Benzaiten Shrine
[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = {{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17|1=July 17}} | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501|1=Tenkawa, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "{{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|en|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|WD=Q17214976|lt=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|1=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}}" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubushima Shrine|1=Chikubu Island}}) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja|1=En no Gyōja}}, who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya|1=Mount Kōya}}. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, who prayed for victory during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh|1=Noh}} theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}}–{{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17|1=July 17}} festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen|1=Mount Misen}} (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From {{ill|Shimoichiguchi Station|en|Shimoichiguchi Station|WD=Q7496967|lt=Shimoichiguchi Station|1=Shimoichiguchi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - {{ill|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|en|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|WD=Q1204533|lt=Yoshino-Kumano National Park|1=Yoshino-Kumano National Park}} == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天河大弁財天社]] {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752}} [[Category:Buildings in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Biwazan Byakuhakuji]] (formerly affiliated with [[Kōfuku-ji]]) | Festival = {{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17|1=July 17}} | location = {{ill|Tenkawa|WD=Q1203501|1=Tenkawa, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.22331248544997|N|135.84170253926723|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine''' (天河大弁財天社), commonly referred to as '''Tenkawa Shrine''', is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[:en:Yoshino District|Yoshino District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The shrine is renowned for its deep association with the arts, music, and spirituality. It is a popular pilgrimage site for those in creative professions and seekers of spiritual insight. == Enshrined deity == The shrine's primary deity is [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]], one of the [[Three Munakata Goddesses|Munakata Three Goddesses]]. Originally, the shrine was dedicated to [[Benzaiten]] (the Japanese Buddhist equivalent of the Hindu goddess [[Saraswati]]). Following the [[Shinto-Buddhism separation]] (神仏分離) during the Meiji period, the deity was renamed to align with Shinto practices while retaining Benzaiten's legacy. The shrine is often considered one of the "{{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|en|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|WD=Q17214976|lt=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|1=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}}" (along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubushima Shrine|1=Chikubu Island}}) and is sometimes included among the "Five Great Benzaiten Shrines of Japan." == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} and is closely associated with [[Shugendō]] practices and the legendary ascetic {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja|1=En no Gyōja}}, who established [[Ōmine-san]] as a sacred site. {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (the founder of [[:en:Kōyasan|Kōyasan]]) also revered the shrine and reportedly spent three years here in spiritual practice before establishing {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya|1=Mount Kōya}}. The shrine's present structure dates back to the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, who prayed for victory during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. Following his triumph, Emperor Tenmu contributed to the shrine’s expansion, solidifying its significance. Historically, the site was referred to as '''Biwazan Byakuhakuji''', a Buddhist temple dedicated to Benzaiten. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was highly regarded as a place of worship for the [[Southern Court of Japan]], further solidifying its status as a spiritual hub. == Cultural Significance == The shrine is known for its unique connection to traditional {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh|1=Noh}} theater and music. It houses numerous offerings related to the performing arts, and its annual {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}}–{{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17|1=July 17}} festival includes sacred performances and rituals. The sacred statue of Benzaiten enshrined in the main hall is normally kept hidden and is only revealed during the annual grand festival. Another sacred statue, known as the "Sun Wheel Benzaiten," is unveiled every 60 years but was last displayed in 2008 as part of a special commemoration. == Sacred Site and Facilities == The shrine's grounds include: - The **Five Shrines Hall**, symbolizing cosmic harmony. - A path leading to the summit of {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen|1=Mount Misen}} (1,895 meters), which serves as the shrine's [[Okumiya|Okunomiya]] (inner sanctuary). == Important Cultural Properties == - **Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks**: A collection of 30 masks designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government. == Festivals and Rituals == Key events include: - **New Year Ceremony (January 1)**: Blessings for the year ahead. - **Misen Inner Shrine Festival (May)**: Rituals performed at the summit of Mount Misen. - **Grand Festival (July 16–17)**: Sacred performances and prayers. - **Autumn Festival (November 2)**: Celebrates harvest and gratitude. == Access == - By bus: From {{ill|Shimoichiguchi Station|en|Shimoichiguchi Station|WD=Q7496967|lt=Shimoichiguchi Station|1=Shimoichiguchi Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]], take the Nara Kotsu Bus to "Tenkawa Daibenzaiten Shrine" (a11rox. 1 hour). - By car: Drive south along [[:en:National Route 309|National Route 309]] and follow signs to the shrine. == See Also == - [[Ōmine Mountains]] - [[Shugendō]] - [[Benzaiten]] - {{ill|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|en|Yoshino-Kumano National Park|WD=Q1204533|lt=Yoshino-Kumano National Park|1=Yoshino-Kumano National Park}} == External Links == * [http://www.tenkawa-jinja.or.jp/ Official website] * [http://www.naraken-jinjacho.jp/jinja13.html Nara Prefecture Shrine Directory] {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|天河大弁財天社|version=100692558|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11442752|co|commons:Category:Tenkawa-dai-Benzaiten-sha|ja|天河大弁財天社}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tenkawa, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Tenmu]] [[Category:En no Gyōja]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenkawa Village]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
